Archive / Issue #46

St. Patrick's Weekend & Maple Season

Wednesday, March 11, 2026

Editor's Note

"There's a specific kind of Ottawa energy that shows up on St. Patrick's Day — everyone a little giddy, Sparks Street louder than usual, the pubs full by noon. It's one of the city's best secular holidays. Lean into it."
Featured This Week

St. Patrick's Day Parade on Sparks Street

Sunday, March 15 · 11:00 AMSparks Street, starting at Elgin

Ottawa's annual St. Patrick's parade winds through Sparks Street with marching bands, Irish dancers, community floats, and more green than you've ever seen in one place. Free to watch, arrive early for a good spot on the south side.

Get Tickets & Details

Getting this from a friend?

Subscribe free and get Ottawa's best in your inbox every Thursday.

Free, every Thursday. Unsubscribe anytime.

This Week's Highlights

Fulton's Maple Sugar Pancake Breakfast

Every weekend through April · 9:00 AM – 3:00 PMFulton's Pancake House, Pakenham

The Gatineau Hills' most famous maple operation is in full swing. Pancake breakfast with fresh syrup, wagon rides through the sugar bush, and a sugar shack tour. One of the best day trips from Ottawa.

More info

Irish Trad Session at the Manx Pub

Saturday, March 14 · 9:00 PM – midnightThe Manx Pub, 370 Elgin St

One of Ottawa's best traditional Irish music sessions happens every second Saturday at the Manx. Local musicians, no cover, genuinely excellent. Get there by 8:30 to find a seat.

More info

NAC English Theatre – 'The Importance of Being Earnest'

Opens March 13, runs through April 5NAC Azrieli Studio, 1 Elgin St

A fresh, modern staging of Wilde's comedy of manners from the NAC English Theatre. Sharp and genuinely funny — this production leans into the absurdity without losing the elegance.

More info

Ottawa Redblacks Fan Fest & Season Preview

Saturday, March 14 · 1:00 PM – 5:00 PMLansdowne Park, South Stand

The CFL season is still months away but the Redblacks host their annual fan event with autograph sessions, a Q&A with the coaching staff, and a look at the new uniforms. Free entry.

More info

Family Corner

Ottawa Public Library – St. Pat's Story Time

Saturday, March 14 · 10:30 AM·Ottawa Public Library, Main Branch, 120 Metcalfe St

Irish folk tales and leprechaun legends for kids aged 3–8. Crafts follow the reading — kids make paper shamrocks and a leprechaun trap. Free, no registration required.

More info

Maple Syrup Workshop for Kids

Sunday, March 15 · 11:00 AM and 2:00 PM·Macnamara Nature Trail, Arnprior

Kids learn how maple sap becomes syrup, tap a tree themselves, and taste fresh sap. Guided by local producers. $12 per family, reservations required.

More info

Rideau River Nature Walk – Spotting Signs of Spring

Sunday, March 15 · 1:00 PM·Hurdman Park, 1035 Innes Rd

A free, family-friendly 45-minute walk along the Rideau with a naturalist who'll point out the first signs of spring — returning birds, pussy willows, and ice-out changes. All ages welcome.

More info
